DataValidator — Data Validation¶

Data validation module for quality assurance before database loading.

Validates fetched data against a set of rules before it enters PostgreSQL. Each validation rule returns a report of issues found, allowing the pipeline to decide whether to proceed, warn, or halt.

Validation rules:

  • Completeness: minimum row counts, no fully-null critical columns

  • Range checks: prices > 0, dates within expected window

  • Consistency: no duplicate (symbol, date) pairs

  • Coverage: percentage of symbols with usable data

class modules.processing.data_validator.ValidationResult[source]¶

Bases: object

Container for validation outcomes.

passed¶

Whether all checks passed.

warnings¶

List of non-critical issues (data is usable but imperfect).

errors¶

List of critical issues (data should not be loaded).

stats¶

Dictionary of summary statistics from the validation.

add_warning(message)[source]¶

Record a non-critical issue.

Parameters:

message – Description of the warning.

add_error(message)[source]¶

Record a critical issue and mark validation as failed.

Parameters:

message – Description of the error.

summary()[source]¶

Return a human-readable summary string.

Returns:

Summary of validation results.

Return type:

str

class modules.processing.data_validator.DataValidator(min_price_rows=200, min_years=4, max_null_pct=0.5)[source]¶

Bases: object

Validates fetched financial data before loading into PostgreSQL.

Runs a configurable set of checks on price data, financial statements, and risk-free rates. Returns a ValidationResult for each dataset so the pipeline can decide how to proceed.

Parameters:

  • min_price_rows – Minimum expected rows per symbol for prices.

  • min_years – Minimum years of price history expected.

  • max_null_pct – Maximum allowed percentage of nulls in critical columns.

validate_prices(df, expected_symbols=None)[source]¶

Validate daily price data.

Checks:

  • DataFrame is not empty

  • No negative or zero prices

  • Date range spans at least min_years

  • Each symbol has at least min_price_rows rows

  • No duplicate (symbol, date) pairs

  • Close price column has acceptable null rate

Parameters:

  • df – Price DataFrame with columns: symbol, price_date, open_price, high_price, low_price, close_price, volume.

  • expected_symbols – Optional list of symbols we expected to get.

Returns:

ValidationResult

validate_financials(df, expected_symbols=None)[source]¶

Validate quarterly financial statement data.

Checks:

  • DataFrame is not empty

  • Total assets are positive where present

  • No duplicate (symbol, fiscal_date) pairs

  • Critical columns have acceptable null rates

  • Reasonable value ranges (no astronomically wrong numbers)

Parameters:

  • df – Financials DataFrame with columns: symbol, fiscal_date, total_assets, total_equity, total_debt, net_income, etc.

  • expected_symbols – Optional list of symbols we expected to get.

Returns:

ValidationResult

validate_risk_free_rates(df, expected_countries=None)[source]¶

Validate risk-free rate data.

Checks:

  • DataFrame is not empty

  • Rates are within reasonable bounds (0-100%)

  • Date range covers recent period

  • No duplicate (country, rate_date) pairs

Parameters:

  • df – Risk-free rates DataFrame with columns: country, rate_date, rate.

  • expected_countries – Optional list of country codes expected.

Returns:

ValidationResult

clean_prices(df)[source]¶

Remove rows with invalid close prices before validation.

Drops rows where close_price is zero or negative. These are data errors (impossible for a real stock) and should not enter the database. Logs a warning so there is an audit trail of what was dropped.

Parameters:

df – Price DataFrame with a close_price column.

Returns:

Cleaned DataFrame with bad rows removed.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

validate_all(prices_df, financials_df, rates_df, expected_symbols=None, expected_countries=None)[source]¶

Run all validations and return a combined report.

Parameters:

  • prices_df – Price data DataFrame.

  • financials_df – Financial statements DataFrame.

  • rates_df – Risk-free rates DataFrame.

  • expected_symbols – Optional list of expected ticker symbols.

  • expected_countries – Optional list of expected country codes.

Returns:

Mapping of dataset name to ValidationResult.

Return type:

dict
